It belongs to the genus Peucedanum, which comprises about 30 species of flowering plants. The species Peucedanum dissolutum is further categorized as a flowering plant within the class Magnoliopsida, also known as dicotyledons. Peucedanum dissolutum is native to the mountainous regions of China, where it grows as a perennial herb. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve digestion, and boost the immune system.
